content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a physical reflection of light and a deliberate cognitive act of reflection, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to integrate two complementary core functions: mirroring the output of prior reasoning steps (the "reflection of light" sense) and actively questioning inconsistencies between conflicting reasoning perspectives to refine output (the "critical self-reflection" sense). Current LLM self-reflection frameworks often overemphasize the critical revision function while underutilizing the straightforward memory-based mirroring function that enables clearer contrast between inconsistent perspectives.
